policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-003, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-005, SEC-RULE-001, SEC-RULE-007, CS-RULE-018, CS-RULE-019, CS-RULE-020, CS-RULE-021, CS-RULE-022]
expected_outcome: Security PIN reset completed on-call after successful 3-of-5 verification (name + account number + last 4 of payment). No ticket required. Case ref provided. Survey offered.

coverage.notes: name + account number + last 4 of payment — PASS

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Priya. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And just so you know, this call may be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hi Priya, my name is Rachel Thornton. I can't log into my account — I think my PIN isn't working anymore.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm sorry to hear that, Ms. Thornton — being locked out is frustrating. I understand you're calling regarding a security PIN reset, and I can certainly help you with that.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay, thank you. I tried it three times and now it just says blocked.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's a standard protective lockout — it doesn't mean anything is wrong with your account. Before I can pull anything up or make any changes, I'll need to verify your identity. Can I start with the account number on file?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Sure. It's five-five-one-two, nine-zero-eight-seven, four-four.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you. And could you confirm the last four digits of the payment method on file?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That should be the card ending in seven-three-one-two.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Perfect, that matches. And to complete the third identifier — could you confirm the full name on the account exactly as it appears?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Rachel Anne Thornton.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Wonderful. You're fully verified, Ms. Thornton — that's three of five identifiers confirmed.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Oh good.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Just a quick note: your data is used solely for service provisioning and support quality, in accordance with our Privacy Policy. I'll proceed with the PIN reset now.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You'll be choosing a new four-digit PIN. It can't be all the same digit, and it can't be a simple sequence like one-two-three-four. Do you have one in mind?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Eight-two-five-one.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Eight-two-five-one. Let me set that now.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm still here with you, Ms. Thornton — the system is just confirming the change. Thank you for your patience.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No problem.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Alright — the new PIN is set and the lockout has been cleared. You should be able to log in immediately.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Should I try it now while you're on the line?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's a great idea. Take your time.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay, I'm in. That worked.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Wonderful. I'm glad that's sorted.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Just to give you the full picture — the lockout happens automatically after three failed PIN attempts. It's a security feature to protect against someone else trying to guess in. Nothing was wrong with your account, and no one else accessed it.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Oh, that's a relief. I was worried someone had hacked me.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Completely understandable, but no — clean lockout, clean reset. To summarize: you reported you were locked out, I verified your identity using three of five identifiers, reset your security PIN to the new value you chose, and confirmed you can log in. There's no follow-up required on your end.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Perfect. Thank you so much, Priya.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: My pleasure. Is there anything else I can help you with today, Ms. Thornton?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, that was the only thing.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your case reference number for today is N-X-L dash six-six-one-nine-four-zero-three. After we hang up you'll receive a brief survey — if you have a moment to share your feedback, we'd really appreciate it.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Sure, happy to.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you so much for being a NexaLink customer, Ms. Thornton. Have a great rest of your day.
